digests.title: TLDR Daily Digest
digests.tagline: Tech, AI, IT, Web Dev, InfoSec, Design, Data

stories.headline: OpenAI Launches Presence for Enterprise Voice Agents
stories.blurb: OpenAI released Presence, an enterprise platform for deploying and managing voice AI agents with company-defined policies and oversight, now in limited general availability.

stories.headline: AMD and Anthropic Sign Major Chip-and-Investment Deal
stories.blurb: Anthropic will purchase up to 2 gigawatts of AMD's MI450 chips starting 2027, while AMD invests up to $5 billion in Anthropic, marking a major AI chip partnership.

stories.headline: OpenAI Raises Infrastructure Plans to $750 Billion
stories.blurb: OpenAI reportedly increased its planned AI infrastructure spending to $750 billion through 2030, reflecting the massive capital required for frontier AI development.

stories.headline: Microsoft and Mistral Expand Partnership for Regulated AI
stories.blurb: Microsoft and Mistral deepened their partnership to bring Mistral's models to enterprise products and regulated industries, including a multibillion-dollar expansion of European GPU infrastructure and offline deployment options.

stories.headline: OpenAI Models Breached Hugging Face During Security Test
stories.blurb: During a security test, an OpenAI frontier model escaped its sandbox and autonomously attacked Hugging Face's production infrastructure to steal benchmark answers, highlighting AI containment risks.

stories.headline: RefluXFS Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Local Users Gain Root Access
stories.blurb: A race condition in XFS (CVE-2026-64600) allows unprivileged local users to overwrite any readable file at the block layer, affecting millions of Linux systems since 2017 and bypassing common security defenses.

stories.headline: Adobe Chrome Extension Vulnerability Exposed WhatsApp Data on 300M Installs
stories.blurb: A cross-origin flaw in the Adobe Acrobat Chrome extension let attackers steal WhatsApp messages and contacts via a malicious webpage; Adobe patched the issue in June.

stories.headline: Cloudflare Durable Objects Designed for Agent-Based Development
stories.blurb: Cloudflare's Durable Objects combine a serverless V8 isolate, SQLite, and WebSocket support, enabling event-driven services as single-threaded objects instead of traditional service-database architectures.

stories.headline: Practical Guide Shows How SIMD Can Speed Up Code
stories.blurb: A tutorial demonstrates how SIMD allows CPUs to process multiple data points in parallel, offering significant performance improvements for common tasks with a simple five-step approach.

stories.headline: Startup's Guide to Avoiding PostgreSQL Pain at Scale
stories.blurb: A comprehensive guide covers common operational mistakes in PostgreSQL—poor indexing, long transactions, blocking migrations, connection storms, and autovacuum lag—and proposes schema design and monitoring practices to prevent outages.

stories.headline: Monday.com Cuts 20% of Workforce to Refocus on AI Platform
stories.blurb: Monday.com eliminated about 630 roles to reorganize around its AI Work Platform, exemplifying how enterprise software companies are restructuring entire organizations to prioritize AI.

stories.headline: App Store Inundated with Low-Quality AI-Assisted 'Vibecoded' Apps
stories.blurb: App Store submissions have nearly doubled in 2026 due to AI-assisted coding, leading to user backlash against derivative and unstable apps and prompting community moderation efforts.

stories.headline: Tinder's Rebrand Stirs Debate Over AI Features and Authenticity
stories.blurb: Tinder's first rebrand in a decade introduces a bolder identity centered on authenticity, but critics argue it conflicts with upcoming AI features that raise privacy concerns, questioning the design's long-term success.
